fault_hypothesis: Lines 65-66 incorrectly remove the last element when both lists end with the same value, but this deduplication logic is wrong — the merged result should always contain len(left)+len(right) elements. The entire if-block on line 65 is a spurious post-processing step that corrupts valid output.

constraints.intent: After the merge loop and extends, merged must have exactly len(left)+len(right) elements before any pop.
After the pop inside the if-branch, the count is wrong — it should still equal len(left)+len(right).
At each loop iteration, the number of merged elements equals the sum of left and right pointers.
The returned merged list must contain exactly all elements from both inputs.
